hero-title: Get AI in 10 Minutes. No Big Words, No Hype.
Understand AI in About 10 Minutes. No Jargon, No Hype.

hero-subtitle: AI is everywhere now. Most explanations are either too hard to follow or too excited. Here is the plain stuff you really need to know.
AI is everywhere these days. Most explanations are either too technical or too breathless. Here's the clear, useful part you actually need to know.
AI is everywhere. Most explanations are either too technical or too breathless. Here's what you actually need to know.
AI is everywhere. Most explanations skew too technical or too breathless. Here's what actually matters.
AI is everywhere. Explanations are too technical or too breathless. Here's what matters.
what-does-1: AI here means large language models, like ChatGPT and Claude. What they really do is guess the next word that should come next, again and again. So they are very good at spotting patterns, but they do not truly think — a difference worth keeping in mind as you weigh what they give you. Behind the scenes, they are very clever systems that have read a huge amount of text.

AI here means large language models like ChatGPT and Claude, and they work by predicting the next most likely word in a sequence. That makes them very good pattern-matchers rather than real thinkers — a difference worth weighing as you read what they give you. Under the hood, each one is a very sophisticated system trained on a vast amount of text.

AI — specifically large language models like ChatGPT and Claude — predicts the next most likely word in a sequence. It's a very good pattern-matcher rather than a thinker — a difference worth keeping in mind as you weigh what it gives you. Under the hood it's an extraordinarily sophisticated system trained on vast amounts of text.

AI — large language models like ChatGPT and Claude — predicts the next likely word in a sequence. It's a strong pattern-matcher, not a thinker — a difference worth weighing as you read its output. Under the hood: a sophisticated system trained on vast text.
LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude) predict the next likely word. Pattern-matchers, not thinkers — worth weighing as you read. Sophisticated systems trained on vast text.
what-does-2: This makes AI genuinely useful for lots of everyday work: writing first drafts, shortening long documents, writing code, and sorting through data. It also has a few honest limits worth knowing about, and they are easy to allow for once you can spot them.

This makes it genuinely useful for many kinds of work: drafting text, summarising documents, generating code, and processing data. It also has a few honest limitations worth knowing — and they're easy to work around once you can spot them.

This makes it genuinely useful for all sorts of work: drafting text, summarising documents, generating code, processing data. It also has a few honest limitations worth knowing — and they're easy to allow for once you can spot them.

This makes it genuinely useful for plenty of work: drafting text, summarising documents, generating code, processing data. It also has honest limitations worth knowing — easy to allow for once you can spot them.
Useful for drafting, summarising, coding, and processing data. Its limits are easy to allow for once you know them.
card-wrong-title: It Can Be Wrong — and Still Sound Sure
It Can Get Things Wrong — Confidently
It can get things wrong — confidently
card-wrong-body: Sometimes AI says things that are not true. The tricky part is that it says them just as smoothly and surely as the correct answers. A quick check from you keeps it honest.
AI sometimes states things that aren't true, and it says them just as smoothly and confidently as the right answers. A quick check from you keeps it honest.
AI sometimes states things that aren't true, and it says them just as smoothly as the right answers. A quick check from you keeps it honest.
AI sometimes states untruths as smoothly as it states facts. A quick check from you keeps it honest.
AI states falsehoods as smoothly as facts. Check its claims.
card-explain-title: It Can't Really Explain Why It Said Something
card-explain-body: When AI "explains" how it got an answer, that explanation is just more text it made up — not a real look inside how it works. It reads more like a helpful draft than the last word.
When AI "explains" its reasoning, that's just more generated text — not a real window into actual logic. It reads more like a helpful draft than the final word.
When AI "explains" its reasoning, that's more generated text — not a window into actual logic. It reads more like a helpful draft than the final word.
When AI "explains" its reasoning, that's more generated text — not a window into real logic. More a draft than the final word.
AI "reasoning" is generated text, not real logic. A draft, not the final word.
card-early-body: These tools are getting better quickly, and things keep changing. There's an upside to that: no one has it all figured out yet, so now is an easy time to get comfortable with AI.
The tools are improving fast and the ground keeps shifting. There's an upside there — no settled expertise to be behind on yet, so now is an easy time to get comfortable.
The tools are improving fast and the ground keeps shifting. There's an upside there — no settled expertise to be behind on yet, so now is an easy time to get comfortable.
The tools improve fast and the ground keeps shifting. The upside: no settled expertise yet, so now is an easy time to get comfortable.
Tools improve fast; the ground shifts. No settled expertise yet — an easy time to start.
card-withyou-title: It Works Best Together With You
card-withyou-body: Used the right way, AI can make your work better — and how much it helps tends to track what you bring: your direction and your good judgement.
Used well, AI can lift the quality of your work — and how much it helps tends to track what you bring: your direction and judgement.
Used well, AI can lift the quality of your work — and how much it helps tends to track what you bring: your direction and judgement.
Used well, AI can lift your work — and how much it helps tends to track what you bring: your direction and judgement.
Used well, AI can lift your work. How much it helps tends to track what you bring: your direction and judgement.
approach-title: A Better Way to Think About It: Augmented Intelligence
approach-1: Instead of using AI to take over human thinking, augmented intelligence uses it to make your thinking stronger. You point the way. AI helps. You check the work. AI writes things. You make the call. AI does the heavy lifting.

Instead of treating AI as a replacement for human thinking, augmented intelligence treats it as an amplifier of your thinking. You direct. AI assists. You verify. AI generates. You decide. AI processes.
Instead of treating AI as a replacement for human thinking, augmented intelligence treats it as an amplifier. You direct. AI assists. You verify. AI generates. You decide. AI processes.
Rather than a replacement for human thinking, augmented intelligence treats AI as an amplifier. You direct. AI assists. You verify. AI generates. You decide. AI processes.
Augmented intelligence amplifies thinking, not replaces it. You direct; AI assists. You verify; AI generates. You decide; AI processes.
approach-2: The argument for it: time you get back, work that improves, and skills that may grow more valuable each year rather than less.
The argument for it: time reclaimed, higher-quality output, and skills that may grow more valuable every year rather than less.
The case for it: reclaimed time, higher-quality output, and skills that may grow more valuable every year rather than less.
The case for it: reclaimed time, better output, and skills that may grow more valuable every year — not less.
The case: time back, better output, skills that may grow more valuable each year.
